title: Actual and Projected Supply and Consumption Patterns of Dairy Products, Iran 1972 and 1977
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank) Agricultural Task Force (IBRD)
source_url: https://openknowledge.worldbank.org (World Bank Archives, Iran country documents)
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION. Source document: data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1974_economic_development_vol4_statistical_appendix.pdf, Table 10.7, page 80 (PDF page 88). Rendered to PNG via pdftoppm -r 200 and visually read. No value interpolated, estimated, or fabricated.
units: product in '000 tons or '000 head of livestock (supply side); fat-equivalent in tons; percent of total fat-equivalent
notes: Splits Iran's dairy consumption into 'high value' (fresh milk, urban yogurt, cream, powdered milk) vs 'low value' (rural yogurt, cheese, butter, butter-oil) products, each converted to a common fat-equivalent basis for comparability -- a genuinely micro-level disaggregation FAOSTAT's aggregate 'Milk - Excluding Butter' balance does not offer. Supply side assumes self-sufficiency and breaks down milk source by animal (pure-bred/cross-bred/local cows, goats, sheep, buffaloes).
retrieved_by: claude (manual transcription with visual verification, pdftoppm -r 200 render + Read tool)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
